req.title: Announcement rollout
req.statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: The requirement has three testable branches: (1) a valid publish with at least one department selected (EP positive path), (2) a publish attempt with zero departments selected which must return a 422 rejection (NEG / DT), and (3) a boundary case where exactly one department is selected — the minimum valid selection (BVA). A fourth test covers the state transition from draft to published to confirm the announcement lifecycle (STC). Together these cases exercise all decision branches in the requirement statement.

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_with_multiple_departments
preconditions: At least two departments exist in the organisation
actions: Fill announcement title and body with valid non-empty text
Select two or more department checkboxes
expected: Announcement appears in the org-announcements list
Target department employees are marked as notified
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_publish_announcement_with_multiple_departments(admin_page, announcement_cleanup):
    page = admin_page
    page.goto("/org-announcements/new")

    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-title']", "All Hands Q3")
    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-body']", "Quarterly update for all staff.")

    depts = page.locator("[data-testid='dept-checkbox']")
    depts.nth(0).check()
    depts.nth(1).check()

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST") as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='publish-btn']")

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status}"

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-toast']")).to_be_visible()
    announcement_cleanup(resp.json()["id"])

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_no_departments_rejected
actions: Fill announcement title and body with valid non-empty text
expected: Inline validation error referencing department selection is visible
trace: R-WEB-087: publishing without any department selection shall be rejected
R-WEB-087: error pathway 422 when no departments selected
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_publish_announcement_no_departments_rejected(admin_page):
    page = admin_page
    page.goto("/org-announcements/new")

    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-title']", "No-Dept Announcement")
    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-body']", "This should be rejected.")

    # Ensure no checkboxes are checked
    for cb in page.locator("[data-testid='dept-checkbox']").all():
        if cb.is_checked():
            cb.uncheck()

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST") as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='publish-btn']")

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status}"

    error_msg = page.locator("[data-testid='dept-error']") 
    expect(error_msg).to_be_visible()
    expect(error_msg).to_contain_text("department")

tests.name: test_publish_announcement_exactly_one_department
preconditions: At least one department exists in the organisation
actions: Fill announcement title and body with valid non-empty text
Select exactly one department checkbox (minimum valid selection)
expected: Only employees in the single selected department are notified
trace: R-WEB-087: BVA lower boundary — exactly one department is the minimum accepted selection
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_publish_announcement_exactly_one_department(admin_page, announcement_cleanup):
    page = admin_page
    page.goto("/org-announcements/new")

    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-title']", "Single Dept Notice")
    page.fill("[data-testid='announcement-body']", "Boundary test: one department only.")

    checkboxes = page.locator("[data-testid='dept-checkbox']")
    assert checkboxes.count() >= 1, "No department checkboxes found"

    # Select exactly one — the minimum valid boundary value
    checkboxes.nth(0).check()
    checked_count = sum(1 for cb in checkboxes.all() if cb.is_checked())
    assert checked_count == 1, f"Expected 1 checked dept, found {checked_count}"

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/org-announcements" in r.url and r.request.method == "POST") as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='publish-btn']")

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status}"
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-toast']")).to_be_visible()
    announcement_cleanup(resp.json()["id"])

tests.name: test_announcement_state_transition_draft_to_published
preconditions: A saved draft announcement with one selected department exists
Browser session is on the draft's edit page
actions: Navigate to the existing draft announcement edit URL
Confirm at least one department is already checked
Click the Publish button to transition state from draft to published
expected: Announcement status changes from draft to published
HTTP 200 PATCH response confirms state change
Published timestamp is set and visible on the detail page
trace: R-WEB-087: target employees notified after state transition
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_announcement_state_transition_draft_to_published(admin_page, create_draft_announcement, announcement_cleanup):
    page = admin_page
    draft = create_draft_announcement(departments=["engineering"])
    draft_id = draft["id"]

    page.goto(f"/org-announcements/{draft_id}/edit")

    status_before = page.locator("[data-testid='announcement-status']").inner_text()
    assert status_before.lower() == "draft", f"Precondition failed: status is '{status_before}'"

    dept_checkboxes = page.locator("[data-testid='dept-checkbox']:checked")
    assert dept_checkboxes.count() >= 1, "Draft must have at least one dept pre-selected"

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: f"/org-announcements/{draft_id}" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PATCH", "PUT")) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='publish-btn']")

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status}"
    assert resp.json()["status"] == "published"

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='announcement-status']")).to_have_text("published", ignore_case=True)
    announcement_cleanup(draft_id)
